title: Phyllobolus latipetalus
taxonomy: Phyllobolus latipetalus is classified as a species of lichen-forming fungus in the family Physciaceae. It belongs to the genus Phyllobolus, which is a group of lichens that are typically crustose or squamulose. The classification of Phyllobolus latipetalus is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. intro: Phyllobolus latipetalus, commonly known as Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The health benefits of Desert Marigold include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues.
